title: Determine Robot Status From Robot LED Colors
summary: Use the documented robot LED colors and flashing patterns to identify the robot's current status. This source-specific reference covers disconnected, in-task, label-searching, idle, and red fault or low-battery indications, and directs the user to the message panel when the robot is red.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to interpret the robot's current status by observing the robot LEDs and, for red status, checking the message panel for additional information.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to infer meanings for undocumented LED colors or patterns.
Do not use this runbook as a full troubleshooting or recovery workflow for robot faults, battery recovery, or communication loss.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe the robot LEDs, identify the displayed color or flashing pattern, interpret the documented meaning, and check the message panel when the robot is red.

safety_notes: Use only the documented LED meanings from the source.
Do not infer undocumented meanings for other colors or patterns.
If the observed LED behavior does not match one of the documented colors or patterns, escalate.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the message panel for red robot conditions

access_required: Ability to visually inspect the robot LEDs
Access to view the message panel
system_preconditions: The robot is visible to the operator so the LED color and flashing state can be observed.

visual_references.description: Training video frame documenting robot LED color meanings and the note that red fault or low battery conditions appear in the message panel.

steps.title: Observe the robot LEDs
steps.purpose: Establish the visible LED indication to interpret robot status.
steps.instruction: Observe the robot LEDs on the robot. The source states there are two LEDs on each side of the robot.
steps.expected_result: The operator can clearly see the robot LED indication.
steps.healthy_condition: The LED indication is visible and can be assessed for color and flashing behavior.
steps.failure_condition: The LEDs cannot be clearly observed or the indication cannot be determined.

actions.label: Observe robot LEDs
actions.exact_action: Observe the robot LEDs on the robot; the source states there are two LEDs on each side of the robot.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot LED color meaning reference in the training frame.

stop_or_escalate_if: The LEDs cannot be seen clearly.
The observed indication cannot be determined.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The artifact explains robot LED color meanings and is the visual reference for this interpretation aid.

steps.title: Identify color and flashing state
steps.purpose: Determine which documented LED state applies.
steps.instruction: Identify the displayed color and whether the indication is solid or flashing.
steps.expected_result: The operator has identified the LED color and whether it is solid or flashing.
steps.healthy_condition: A documented color or flashing pattern is recognized.
steps.failure_condition: The color or flashing pattern does not match the documented states.

actions.label: Identify LED state
actions.exact_action: Identify the displayed color and whether the indication is solid or flashing.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Documented LED colors and distinction between solid blue and flashing blue.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed LED behavior does not match one of the documented colors or patterns.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Robot colors: Purple = robot disconnected; Blue/Flashing Blue = robot in task/searching for label; Green = robot not in a task; Red = fault/low battery.

steps.title: Interpret purple LED
steps.purpose: Map the purple LED state to the documented robot condition.
steps.expected_result: A purple LED is recognized as a disconnected robot.
steps.healthy_condition: Purple LED is interpreted as disconnected, consistent with the source.
steps.failure_condition: Purple is interpreted as something other than disconnected.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Purple LED meaning in the robot color reference.

stop_or_escalate_if: Purple is observed but the condition appears inconsistent with the documented meaning.
Additional LED behavior is present that is not documented in the source.

steps.title: Interpret solid blue LED
steps.purpose: Map the blue LED state to the documented robot condition.
steps.instruction: Interpret blue as robot in task.
steps.expected_result: A blue LED is recognized as the robot being in task.
steps.healthy_condition: Solid blue is interpreted as in task, consistent with the source.
steps.failure_condition: Blue is interpreted as something other than in task.

actions.label: Interpret blue
actions.exact_action: Interpret blue as robot in task.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Blue LED meaning in the robot color reference.

stop_or_escalate_if: Blue is observed but the condition appears inconsistent with the documented meaning.

steps.title: Interpret flashing blue LED
steps.purpose: Map the flashing blue LED state to the documented robot condition.
steps.instruction: Interpret flashing blue as the robot looking for a label.
steps.expected_result: A flashing blue LED is recognized as the robot searching for a label.
steps.healthy_condition: Flashing blue is interpreted as looking for a label, consistent with the source.
steps.failure_condition: Flashing blue is interpreted as something other than label searching.

actions.label: Interpret flashing blue
actions.exact_action: Interpret flashing blue as the robot looking for a label.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Flashing blue LED meaning in the robot color reference.

stop_or_escalate_if: Flashing blue is observed but the condition appears inconsistent with the documented meaning.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Flashing Blue = robot searching for label.

steps.title: Interpret green LED
steps.purpose: Map the green LED state to the documented robot condition.
steps.instruction: Interpret green as the robot being idle or not in a task.
steps.expected_result: A green LED is recognized as the robot being idle or not in a task.
steps.healthy_condition: Green is interpreted as idle or not in a task, consistent with the source.
steps.failure_condition: Green is interpreted as something other than idle or not in a task.

actions.label: Interpret green
actions.exact_action: Interpret green as the robot being idle or not in a task.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Green LED meaning in the robot color reference.

stop_or_escalate_if: Green is observed but the condition appears inconsistent with the documented meaning.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Green = robot not in a task.

steps.title: Interpret red LED
steps.purpose: Map the red LED state to the documented robot condition.
steps.instruction: Interpret red as the robot having a fault or a low battery.
steps.expected_result: A red LED is recognized as indicating either a fault or a low-battery condition.
steps.healthy_condition: Red is interpreted as fault or low battery, consistent with the source.
steps.failure_condition: Red is interpreted as something other than fault or low battery.

actions.label: Interpret red
actions.exact_action: Interpret red as the robot having a fault or a low battery.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Red LED meaning in the robot color reference.

stop_or_escalate_if: Red is observed but the condition appears inconsistent with the documented meaning.

steps.title: Check the message panel for red status
steps.purpose: Use the source-supported follow-up reference for red robot conditions.
steps.instruction: If the robot is red, check the message panel because the source states both fault and low battery will show up there.
steps.expected_result: The operator uses the message panel to obtain additional information for a red robot condition.
steps.healthy_condition: The message panel shows the relevant fault or low-battery information for the red robot condition.
steps.failure_condition: The robot is red but the message panel does not provide matching information, or the operator cannot access the message panel.

actions.label: Check message panel
actions.exact_action: If the robot is red, check the message panel because the source states both fault and low battery will show up there.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference stating that red fault or low-battery conditions show up in the message panel.
Message panel area and related message/fault display context.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot is red and the message panel cannot be accessed.
The robot is red and the message panel does not show a matching fault or low-battery indication.
The observed condition does not match the documented source behavior.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Red means the robot has fault or a low battery, both of which will show up in the message panel.
The message panel can show pending errors or warnings and alarm history.

success_criteria: The operator can identify the documented robot status from the observed LED color or flashing pattern.
If the robot is red, the operator knows to check the message panel for additional information.
healthy_conditions: Purple is interpreted as robot disconnected.
Blue is interpreted as robot in task.
Flashing blue is interpreted as robot looking for a label.
Green is interpreted as robot idle or not in a task.
Red is interpreted as robot fault or low battery, with additional information available in the message panel.
failure_conditions: The observed LED behavior does not match one of the documented colors or patterns.
The operator attempts to infer undocumented meanings for other colors or patterns.
The robot is red but the message panel cannot be checked or does not provide matching information.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed LED behavior does not match one of the documented colors or patterns.
Escalate if the robot is red and the message panel cannot be accessed or does not show corresponding fault or low-battery information.
Do not infer undocumented meanings for other colors or patterns.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot LED color meanings and note about red status appearing in the message panel.
Message panel context for viewing errors, warnings, and faults.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Robot colors: Purple = robot disconnected; Blue/Flashing Blue = robot in task/searching for label; Green = robot not in a task; Red = fault/low battery.
Red means the robot has fault or a low battery, both of which will show up in the message panel.

missing_details: The source does not provide a full troubleshooting or recovery workflow for each LED state.
The source does not provide role boundaries beyond operator-level use.
The source does not provide estimated completion time.
The source does not define required production stop or LOTO conditions for this reference procedure.
The source does not specify exact message panel navigation steps.
